commit.message:
Add pre-computed summary/ranking documents for analytical queries

Creates ~10 summary documents per generation during ingestion:
- BST rankings (overall, non-legendary)
- Legendary/Mythical Pokemon list
- Individual stat rankings (HP, Atk, Def, SpAtk, SpDef, Speed)
- Type distribution per generation

These docs are indexed in ChromaDB and found via semantic search
for questions like "qual è il pokemon più forte in platino" that
previously failed because the retriever couldn't surface the right
documents without a specific Pokemon name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: override ~/.claude/settings.json env vars for multi-provider support

Problem:
- When using non-default providers (e.g., bailian), requests were hitting
  the wrong endpoint because ~/.claude/settings.json env vars (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL,
  ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN) were overriding subprocess environment variables
- Linux getenv() returns the first match when duplicate env keys exist,
  so inherited env vars took precedence over our custom values

Solution (three-layer override):
1. filterEnv(): Remove ANTHROPIC_* vars from os.Environ() before appending new ones
2. buildSettingsJSON(): Pass {"env":{...}} via --settings CLI flag to override
   ~/.claude/settings.json (higher precedence than user settings)
3. --model flag: Highest priority, passed directly to claude CLI

Changes:
- internal/claude/executor.go:
  - Add filterEnv() to strip conflicting env vars from inherited environment
  - Add buildSettingsJSON() to generate --settings JSON for non-default providers
  - Update buildArgs() to include --settings and --model flags
  - Update Execute() to filter env before appending provider-specific vars
- internal/claude/executor_test.go:
  - Add TestFilterEnv
  - Add TestBuildSettingsJSON (3 test cases)
  - Update existing tests for new provider resolution logic
- internal/config/config.go:
  - Restructure ClaudeConfig: add DefaultProvider and Providers map
  - Add ProviderConfig struct (BaseURL, AuthToken, Model)
  - Update AppClaudeConfig: remove auth_token/base_url, keep Provider/Model
- internal/config/config_test.go:
  - Add TestLoad_ProviderModelConfig
- docs/design.md:
  - Update config example to show providers structure
  - Add design decision: "多供应商 env 覆盖"
- CLAUDE.md:
  - Document --model and --settings CLI flags
  - Explain three-layer override mechanism
- init_workspace.sh:
  - Add # provider: comment to generated app block
- README.md:
  - Document providers config structure
  - Add supported models table (Anthropic + bailian)
- config.yaml.template:
  - Update to new providers structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
NavAutopilot: fix route-edit loop via capture state reset and heading freeze

Fix A (ActLegsView): call nav.ResetCaptureState() after CommitRoute so stale
wasNear/nearFrames/prevDist state from the previous leg does not immediately
trigger a spurious waypoint advance after a route rebuild.

Fix B (NavAutopilot): freeze targetHdgDeg to the aircraft's current yaw when
waypoints are empty/null and NAV is engaged, preventing a stale intercept
command from being held during the coroutine-based rebuild gap.

Also adds NavAutopilot.ResetCaptureState() public method.

commit.message:
Komplet optimering: ATR SL, regime detection, cycle trading, scale-in

P1 Risk: ATR-baseret stop-loss (2xATR clamped 3-8%), break-even +1.5%,
trailing +2.5% @ 1.5xATR, max hold time (24/8/4h) - kun losers lukkes.

P2 Sizing: Confidence multiplier (6:25%, 7:50%, 8:75%, 9-10:100%),
scale-in ved stigende conviction (+2), 50% af original position.

P3 Exit: Early exit paa 3+ accelererende adverse candles, dynamisk TP
udvidelse (max 2x, 1.5xATR), cycle trading med 30min cooldown.

P4 Regime: ADX(14) regime detector (trending/ranging/neutral), shorts
kun i RANGING/TRENDING_DOWN, MACD divergence +2, volume climax +2,
failed breakout +3, memecoin sell zone 75%.

Ny /ai_debug kommando. AI prompt opdateret med regime og MACD data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix CORS: switch n8n fetch calls to URLSearchParams to avoid preflight

n8n Cloud returns 500 on OPTIONS preflight requests, blocking all POST
requests with JSON bodies from GitHub Pages. Switching to URLSearchParams
(application/x-www-form-urlencoded) sends a simple request with no preflight,
bypassing the CORS block entirely. Affects updateSaleStatus, saveNotes,
and sendFarewellMessage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 12–18 + Kardashev ML: gap fixes, menu v2, synthetic training

Phase 12–17 (previously uncommitted):
- BC11 pattern analysis hardening (Bonferroni, harmonics, non-grav bounds)
- API layer: real persistence, monitoring, batch detection, orbital history
- Data layer: SBDB nongrav fix, observation arc wiring, Horizons history
- Menu redesign: ANEOSMenuV2 (14 options), ANEOSMenuBase helpers
- σ validation integrity audit; honest docstrings; Wilson CIs

Phase 18 — Menu gap analysis + fixes:
- GAP-1/2: aneos_api.services → aneos_api.database; save_analysis_result dict fix
- GAP-3: _show_db_results uses dict .get() (not getattr); column names updated
- GAP-4: run_200_year_poll passes interactive=False (no EOF crash)
- GAP-5: print() in _detect_batch → show_info/show_error
- GAP-7: evidence_breakdown added to DetectionResult.analysis dict
- GAP-8: _parse_elements_table rewritten with epoch accumulation (2 epochs → 2 rows)
- GAP-9: PatternAnalysisConfig harmonics=True
- GAP-10: format_sigma σ<2 → INCONCLUSIVE (removes NOTABLE/ROUTINE)
- M-1/2/3: Options 12–14 added (_start_api_server, _detection_analytics, _show_help)

Kardashev Synthetic ML (new):
- aneos_core/datasets/kardashev_generator.py — 14 scenarios K0.5–K2.0
  (dead_rocket_stage, solar_sail, active_ion_probe, mining_tug,
   deep_space_vehicle, automated_constructor, resource_platform,
   dyson_swarm_element, interstellar_launcher, stellar_trawler,
   population_coordinator, megastructure_fragment,
   stellar_engine_component, network_statistical_anomaly)
- aneos_core/datasets/kardashev_training.py — IsolationForest + RandomForest
  pipeline; hardcoded ground-truth set (2020 SO, J002E3, WT1190F, Roadster-type,
  Apophis, Bennu, Eros, Toutatis, Itokawa, 2001 YB5)
- CONFIRMED BENEFIT: RF AUC=1.000; separation=+0.870; all confirmed artificials
  score >0.94 P(artificial); all confirmed naturals score <0.46
- Primary discriminating feature: log10_density (hollow vs rocky), then albedo

Tests: 235 pass, 0 fail (+15 phase18 + 15 kardashev-gen + 11 kardashev-training)
README updated to v1.0.0 Phase 18.

libraries via the Plugin API, with distinct paths for Team Libraries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: run shell context menu on STA thread to load all extension handlers

Shell extension handlers (7-Zip, Notepad++, etc.) are in-process STA
COM servers. When IContextMenu is created from an MTA thread (the .NET
console app default), COM marshals calls across apartments and the
handlers silently refuse to load, producing only the generic built-in
menu. Fix: dispatch ShowCore() onto a dedicated STA thread via
Thread.SetApartmentState(STA) so all registered handlers load and
appear correctly.

commit.message:
fix: critical memory safety issue in HttpClient.formatBytes

CRITICAL BUG FIX — Memory Safety Violation

Root cause: formatBytes() returned pointer to stack-allocated buffer
- Function used local var buf: [32]u8 = undefined
- Returned slice pointing to this stack memory
- Caused undefined behavior when buffer went out of scope
- Violated core library principle: no memory safety issues

Fix: Proper allocator-based memory management
- Changed formatBytes to accept Allocator parameter
- Use allocPrint instead of bufPrint for heap allocation
- Caller now owns returned memory and must free it
- Added proper defer statements for cleanup

Impact:
- Prevents potential crashes and corruption
- Ensures memory safety in download stats display
- All tests passing, cross-platform builds verified

Severity: HIGH — Library code must never have UB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(blacklist): stop re-backfilling confirmed-zero balance events

The backfill query retried events where amount = 0 on every cycle,
wasting ~1,400 subrequests per run on USDC addresses that genuinely
hold zero balance (Circle's preemptive multi-chain blacklisting).
balanceOf returns null on failure and 0 on success, so 0 is a
confirmed result that needs no retry.

commit.message:
P0 fix: _stitch_course_structure was silently dropping populated content

Root cause: When the LLM skeleton nested lessons inside modules directly,
_stitch_course_structure skipped those modules ("if module.get('lessons'):
continue"). The populated content in course_data["lessons"] was never
stitched into the modules, so the Pydantic model read empty skeleton
lessons. Result: 49 slides instead of ~260.

Fix: Always replace module["lessons"] with populated versions from the
flat lessons array, regardless of whether the module already has lessons.

Also reverted PKE_MAX_TOKENS from 64000 back to 32000 — the larger
token budget was producing oversized skeletons.

4 new regression tests in test_stitch_structure.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
